title: Intensely Nutty French Scallops Gratin
description: This dish features pan seared scallops in a rich and creamy nutty sauce, topped with a layer of caramelized nuts and mitsuba, all while being whole30 friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prepare a bain marie by filling a large baking dish with hot water.
Pan sear the scallops in ghee until browned on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the scallops to a baking dish and set aside.
In the same pan, add the chopped macadamia nuts and cook until fragrant and lightly browned.
Add the chopped mitsuba, garlic, salt, and black pepper to the pan and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the coconut cream and lemon juice and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Pour the nutty sauce over the scallops and place the baking dish in the bain marie.
Bake for 12-15 minutes or until the scallops are cooked through and the top is lightly browned.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
